text:
Journal & Topics Media Group | Serving Chicago's Great Northwest Suburbs

Saturday, December 6, 2025

Members of the Glenview Police Department take part in the Cop on a Rooftop fundraiser on May 17. (Photo by George LeClaire)

Glenview police took up tactical positions on the roof, in the drive-thru, and in the lobby of the Dunkin Donuts at Lake and Milwaukee avenues on Friday, May 17, raising $4,100 for Illinois Special Olympics.Glenview officers joined officers at nearly two dozen other locations across the Northwest suburbs participating in the Cop on a Rooftop fundraiser.Glenview officers began their watch at Lake and Milwaukee at 5 a.m. May 17, remaining at the scene until about noon. One donor reportedly gave a single $200 donation to Illinois Special Olympics.Officers will be running in the upcoming Law Enforcement Torch Run for Illinois Special Olympics, receiving the Torch of Hope at Milwaukee and Lake avenues and running it down to hand off to Niles officers at Milwaukee and Greenwood.If you like this story, you can get a whole lot more practically every day of the week by subscribing to journal-topics.com.Click hereto choose your preference of either print or online, or call 847-299-5511.Support local news by subscribing to the Journal & Topicsin printoronline.
